# TenantScript

> TenantScript is a Cloudflare-native, self-hosted OSS control plane for running tenant-scoped
> plugins, webhook transformations, approval workflows, notification rules, and API policies with
> explicit capabilities, audit evidence, versioning, and rollback.

This file is a compact map for coding agents and contributors. Follow the linked public repository
sources instead of inferring architecture or live deployment details.

## Start here

- [Documentation by audience and verification status](docs/README.md)
- [Project status and documentation map](README.md)
- [Contribution workflow and quality gates](CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
- [Good-first-issue pipeline](docs/community/good-first-issues.md)
- [Phase 2 gate evidence](docs/reviews/phase2-gate-evidence.md)
- [Repository agent guardrails](AGENTS.md)
- [Security reporting policy](SECURITY.md)
- [Product direction and decisions](docs/Cloudflare-native_SaaS_Extension_Control_Plane_Product_Document.md)
- [Phase plan and package boundaries](tasks/README.md)
- [Architecture Decision Records](docs/adr/README.md)

## Runtime posture

TenantScript targets TypeScript on Cloudflare Workers. Dynamic Workers is the preferred Phase 0
candidate, but the final runtime decision is blocked until paid-plan live benchmark evidence is
available. Local tests, workerd-backed security tests, dry-run deployment, and Tier 1 CI remain
accountless. Do not claim the runtime ADR is accepted or invent live performance results.

Use these sources for the current boundary:

- [Runtime primitive ADR](docs/adr/001-runtime-primitive.md)
- [Phase 0 benchmark status](docs/benchmarks/phase0.md)
- [Security suite threat map](docs/security/security-suite-v2.md)
- [Known environment limitations](README.md#既知の環境制約)

Quickstart snippets are executable contracts. Update their linked tests in the same change; do not
copy snippets into a second source of truth.

## Public code entrypoints

- [Manifest parsing and grant resolution](packages/manifest/src/index.ts)
- [Host integration SDK](packages/host-sdk/src/index.ts)
- [Plugin authoring SDK](packages/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts)
- [Sandboxed plugin loader](packages/loader/src/index.ts)
- [Capability broker](packages/capabilities/src/index.ts)
- [Control-plane API and domain behavior](packages/control-plane/src/index.ts)
- [Zero-integration proxy](packages/proxy/src/index.ts)
- [Developer CLI](packages/cli/src/index.ts)
- [Example SaaS composition root](apps/example-saas/src/index.ts)
- [Admin UI composition root](apps/admin-ui/src/App.tsx)

Import public package exports. Do not reach into another workspace's private source path or reverse
the dependency direction documented in the phase plan.

## Safe contribution workflow

Use GitHub Flow from `main` and keep one issue per focused pull request. Behavior changes follow
RED -> GREEN -> REFACTOR: prove the missing behavior with a failing test, implement the smallest
complete contract, then improve structure while tests stay green. Security boundaries require
adversarial coverage for tenant isolation, capabilities, secrets, egress, approvals, and audit.

```sh
# cwd: repository root
# expected-exit: 0
corepack enable
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm verify
git diff --check
```

`pnpm verify` is the canonical local gate: typecheck, lint, behavior tests, coverage, security
tests, high-severity dependency audit, and formatting. Resolve every actionable review thread and
wait for the required `accountless quality gate` before merge.

## Credential and live-test boundary

Tier 1 is fork-safe and must not require Cloudflare, npm, or GitHub write credentials. Tier 2 live
checks use maintainer-controlled credentials and are not required for fork pull requests. Do not
add credentials, account IDs, customer data, private URLs, database dumps, or machine-local paths
to code, fixtures, logs, issues, or documentation. Never convert a missing paid account or npm
scope into a local test bypass.
